turns.note: Proposes a one-page structured study-notes card on the intro deck (source_mode file); the full notes body delivered at Turn 2 is grounded in the deck (definitions, scope, grading 5/40/35/20, open-book policy), so judged on params plus that content.
All three definitions are word-for-word from slide 2 (verified against the corpus), and the rest of the notes (prerequisites, PA oral 60/written 40, not-covered list) stays grounded.
Everything except the three definitions rewritten in plain student language; definitions untouched, grading percentages and the two-modality (text+images) focus preserved, nothing invented.

Adds a highlighted NOT-covered box with exactly slide 3's content (convolutions+attention, CNNs/RNNs/LSTMs/transformers, train/evaluate/build/deploy); prior notes and exact definitions preserved, still about a page.
Exactly three deck-grounded self-check questions (VLM vs LLM, MLLM modalities, not-covered items) appended with no answers anywhere in the student notes.
Separate teacher answer sheet answers the same three questions correctly per the deck (VLM = LLM + vision encoder generating text; MLLM modalities text/images/video/audio); student notes untouched.

Trims to a tighter one-pager without touching the definitions; the not-covered content survives (merged into an in/out-of-scope section) and the three questions stay answer-free, though the trim is done silently without stating what was condensed.
Names 01_dvlm_introduction.pptx as the only file used and inventories both deliverables (one-page student notes with verbatim definitions + separate answer sheet) accurately.

highlight: Fazah held the LLM/VLM/MLLM definitions word-for-word through a simplify pass, a box insertion, question additions, and a trim, while keeping every answer out of the student copy — the cleanest sustained-edit run in this batch.
